questions.question: Explain why the naive solution to the Dining Philosophers problem can lead to deadlock.
expectedPoints: All philosophers pick up left chopstick simultaneously
Each waits for right chopstick held by neighbor
No philosopher can proceed, causing deadlock

questions.question: Compare and contrast the Producer-Consumer and Readers-Writers problems, highlighting their key differences in synchronization requirements.
expectedPoints: Producer-Consumer involves two process types with producer/consumer relationship
Readers-Writers involves two process types with different access patterns
Producer-Consumer needs buffer management with empty/full conditions
Readers-Writers allows concurrent reading but exclusive writing
Different semaphore structures required for each solution
